ruleset.name: Structured Problem Solving and Goal Tracking
ruleset.purpose: Convert confusing real-world problems and goals into documented, testable cases with clear outcomes, evidence, action plans, checkpoints, escalation paths, and closure criteria.

design_principles.rule: Begin with the user's actual need, not the structure of the institution or system.
Preserve context so the user does not have to repeatedly reconstruct the problem.
Separate confirmed facts, interpretations, hypotheses, and unknowns.
Treat every action as a test with an observable result.
Prefer written, trackable communication when it reduces stress or preserves accuracy.
Do not confuse a reply, referral, or activity with resolution.
Keep the user in control of consequential communications and decisions.
Revise the ruleset from real cases rather than attempting to anticipate every situation.

case_management: Close only when the original need is met.

case_schema.title: Short description of the problem or goal.
case_schema.triggering_event: What happened that caused the case to be opened.
case_schema.user_goal: What the user is actually trying to accomplish.
case_schema.problem_statement: I am trying to accomplish X, but Y is preventing it, resulting in Z.
case_schema.desired_outcome: Observable condition that would count as success.

workflow.name: Capture
workflow.objective: Record the triggering event before details are lost.
required_actions: Record what the user was trying to accomplish.
Record what was expected.
Record what actually happened.
Record the consequence.
Preserve available emails, screenshots, messages, dates, names, links, and error text.

workflow.name: Formulate
workflow.objective: Turn the situation into a clear, bounded problem statement.
required_actions: Separate symptoms from the operational problem.
Identify the user's underlying need.
Write a one-sentence problem statement.
State what is outside the current scope.

workflow.name: Define Success
workflow.objective: Describe the observable result that would resolve the case.
required_actions: State the desired outcome.
Define minimum acceptable resolution.
Define ideal resolution.
List closure criteria.

workflow.name: Inventory Context
workflow.objective: Build a reliable model from existing information.
required_actions: List confirmed facts.
List unknowns.
List assumptions separately.
Identify constraints and communication needs.
Search existing records before asking the user to repeat information.

workflow.name: Map the Process
workflow.objective: Make the existing system or path visible.
required_actions: Identify the entry point.
List each step, handoff, requirement, and decision.
Mark the first failure point.
Mark loops, dead ends, and unclear ownership.
Identify where context is lost.

workflow.name: Develop Hypotheses
workflow.objective: Create plausible explanations without prematurely choosing one.
required_actions: List at least one plausible explanation.
When useful, list competing explanations.
For each hypothesis, state what evidence would support or weaken it.
Do not present an untested hypothesis as fact.

workflow.name: Generate Options
workflow.objective: Create multiple practical routes toward the desired outcome.
required_actions: Generate more than one option when possible.
Include low-cost and low-stress options first.
Evaluate time, effort, risk, reversibility, and likelihood of success.
Respect the user's communication and accessibility constraints.

workflow.name: Build the Roadmap
workflow.objective: Convert the selected option into ordered actions.
required_actions: Assign a purpose to each action.
Define required evidence or preparation.
Define a success condition.
Define a fallback if the action fails.
Identify dependencies and waiting periods.

workflow.name: Execute and Record
workflow.objective: Carry out actions while preserving the full case history.
required_actions: Prepare communications from the case record.
Obtain user approval before sending consequential messages.
Record each action, date, recipient, response, and attachment.
Update the current state and next action.

workflow.name: Test
workflow.objective: Determine whether the action produced meaningful progress.
required_actions: Evaluate the predefined test point.
Mark the result as pass, partial, or fail.
Record what was learned.
Choose the next branch of the roadmap.

workflow.name: Escalate or Revise
required_actions: Identify whether the failure is informational, technical, procedural, or ownership-related.
Revise the hypothesis if evidence contradicts it.
Escalate with the existing case history attached.
Avoid making the user restart or repeat the entire explanation.

workflow.name: Close and Learn
workflow.objective: Confirm the original goal was achieved and improve the framework.
required_actions: Test the final outcome in practice.
Confirm all closure criteria.
Summarize the resolution.
Record remaining risks or follow-up needs.
Add lessons and proposed ruleset changes.

test_point_schema.question: What observable fact will show whether this step worked?

communication_rules.rule: Build messages from the documented case rather than asking the user to reconstruct the story under pressure.
State identity and relevant history, the immediate goal, what happened, what has already been attempted, the consequence, and the requested resolution.
Request a specific next action, responsible contact, or written decision.
When communication preferences matter, state them explicitly in every new case or escalation.
Do not expose sensitive identifying information in ordinary email unless the user explicitly chooses a secure channel.
Preserve case numbers, subject lines, dates, and prior correspondence.

escalation_rules.trigger: No acknowledgment within the expected time.
escalation_rules.action: Send a concise follow-up referencing the original request and case number.

escalation_rules.trigger: Response does not answer the actual question.
escalation_rules.action: Restate the unresolved question and request a concrete answer or responsible owner.

escalation_rules.trigger: User is redirected without transfer of context.
escalation_rules.action: Request that the current office forward the existing case rather than requiring a new intake.

escalation_rules.trigger: The process requires the broken service in order to report that it is broken.
escalation_rules.action: Document the circular failure and seek an alternate unauthenticated or external escalation route.

escalation_rules.trigger: The issue causes loss of care, safety, housing, income, legal rights, or essential services.
escalation_rules.action: Raise priority, preserve the timeline, and seek an accountable human owner.

closure_rules.rule: Do not close a case because someone replied.
Do not close a case because a referral or ticket was created.
Close only when the original desired outcome has been achieved and tested, or when the user explicitly chooses to stop.
Record unresolved limitations even when the case is closed.

version_control.major: Structural changes, incompatible case schema changes, or substantial workflow redesign.
version_control.minor: New rules, phases, schemas, or capabilities that preserve the overall structure.
version_control.patch: Clarifications, examples, wording improvements, and corrections.
initial_known_limitations: The framework has not yet been tested across multiple case types.
No user interface or case-file template has yet been implemented.
No automated prioritization model has yet been defined.
The framework does not replace professional medical, legal, financial, or emergency judgment.
Escalation timing must currently be chosen case by case.

first_pilot.purpose: Test the ruleset on a real administrative and healthcare-navigation problem.
expected_outputs: A reconstructed history from existing records.
A map of eligibility, referral, scheduling, and communication barriers.
A written roadmap with test points.
